{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided, a 27-year-old pregnant woman with a thyroid nodule suggestive of papillary carcinoma would typically require a total thyroidectomy to ensure complete removal of the cancer. Total thyroidectomy is the standard of care for papillary thyroid carcinoma, especially in pregnant women, to prevent recurrence and metastasis. Right lobectomy alone would be insufficient as it does not address the entire thyroid gland. Radioactive iodine ablation is also contraindicated during pregnancy as it can harm the developing fetus. Therefore, the only option that is not contraindicated is total thyroidectomy plus neck node dissection.", "answer_choice": "C"}
